title: Smoky Tangy Dutch Sauerkraut Casserole
description: A hearty, fermented Dutch casserole that marries smoky turkey with tangy sauerkraut, thickened by a savory blend of spices and a touch of nutritional yeast for a cheesy, glutenfree finish.
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Lightly grease a 9x13 inch casserole dish with olive oil.
In a skillet, heat 1 tbsp olive oil over medium heat. Add diced onion and garlic, sauté until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add smoked turkey breast, cut into bite‑sized pieces, and sauté until lightly browned, about 5 minutes. Stir in smoked paprika, dried thyme, and black pepper; cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Transfer turkey mixture to the casserole dish. Spread the drained sauerkraut evenly over the turkey.
In a small bowl, whisk together chicken broth, apple cider vinegar, and the remaining 1 tbsp olive oil. Pour this liquid over the sauerkraut and turkey.
Sprinkle nutritional yeast over the top for a cheesy, gooey finish.
Cover the dish with foil and bake in the preheated oven for 25 minutes. Remove foil and bake for an additional 5 minutes, or until the casserole is bubbly and the top begins to brown.
Let the casserole rest for 5 minutes before serving; steam in the residual heat. Garnish with fresh parsley if desired.
